Description: Any `unsafe` use in production code lacking a preceding `// SAFETY:` comment or `/// # Safety` docstring (rustdoc block searched to arbitrary depth). Projection: `cargo-toml-projector` walks every `*.rs` file under `rs/` whose path contains `/src/` and is NOT under `/tests/`, `/benches/`, or `/target/`. Feature-gated code is ALWAYS scanned — the projector reads source text, not the compiled view, so `#[cfg(feature = "X")]` blocks are never invisible. Per-file cutoff fires only when `#[cfg(test)]` gates a module (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }`), NOT when it gates a single fn — a bug caught by external review 2026-07-14 (control.rs's per-fn `#[cfg(test)]` on a test-only helper had been hiding 8 feature-gated interrupt-accessor unsafe blocks). Type-level `unsafe fn(...)` in transmute-target types and Rust 2024 `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` attributes are filtered too. Bead `ley-line-open-85fb1f`. Baseline in `docs/smell-baseline.json`; the gate is at zero — any new unannotated `unsafe` fails CI immediately, no allowlist exceptions.
Query: SELECT u.source_id, u.node_id, u.start_row, u.start_col, u.end_row, u.end_col, 1 AS metric FROM unsafe_sites u WHERE u.has_safety = 0 %s ORDER BY u.source_id, u.start_row
